title: 10 best herbal creams for chickenpox

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the active ingredient in pot marigold cream, has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief to affected areas.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold cream's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may occur as a result of scratching or breaking the skin due to chickenpox, reducing the risk of secondary infections.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula has been shown to promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and enhancing the formation of new skin, helping to reduce scarring and promote faster recovery from chickenpox-related skin lesions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream can help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness associated with chickenpox. Its natural antiseptic properties can also prevent infection and promote wound healing, which is crucial in managing the spread of chickenpox. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help alleviate the discomfort and distress caused by this viral infection. This can aid in the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms.

Flavonoids (including rutin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing the viral load and alleviating the symptoms of chickenpox.

Carotenoids (including beta-carotene): These antioxidants help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, reducing the risk of complications such as scarring and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation associated with chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the skin.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing the severity of chickenpox symptoms.
Stems: Stems are used due to their ability to help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_recipe: Infuse calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Melt a combination of beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and mix until well combined.
Pour the calendula cream mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and set.

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory effects: Aloe vera's active compound, aloin, helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, providing quick relief and promoting faster healing.

Antiviral properties: The antimicrobial agents present in aloe vera cream have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, thereby reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Wound-healing and skin-regenerating capabilities: Aloe vera's rich antioxidant content and mucilages help stimulate collagen production, promote tissue repair, and accelerate wound closure, which is especially important for healing painful blisters and rashes caused by chickenpox.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with chickenpox because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the plant reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with the itchy rash. Aloe vera's moisturizing effects also help to calm dry, irritated skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring. By applying aloe vera cream to affected areas, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and promote a smoother recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms and promote healing of skin lesions.
Vitamins (A, C, and E): These antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.

Saponins: These natural detergents have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the risk of secondary infections and promoting faster healing of chickenpox lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain gel-like substance that is used to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an effective ingredient in creams for chickenpox.
Leaves (Aloe vera gel): Aloe vera gel has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce itching, swelling, and the risk of infection associated with chickenpox.

Leaves (Aloe vera juice): Aloe vera juice is used in creams for chickenpox due to its ability to moisturize and calm the skin, reducing the risk of complications such as scarring and skin discoloration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 3 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a bowl.
Mix the aloe vera gel and coconut oil thoroughly for 2 minutes until well combined.
Add 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 5 minutes or until the shea butter melts.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, making it more comfortable for patients to manage their symptoms.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile cream help prevent bacterial or fungal infections from developing in the skin, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster healing of sores and blisters.

Calming and soothing: Chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress caused by chickenpox, allowing patients to rest and recover more comfortably while also providing a sense of relief from itching and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The soothing properties of chamomile can help reduce redness and irritation associated with chickenpox, providing relief to itchy and uncomfortable skin. Additionally, chamomile has antiviral properties that may help combat the herpes zoster virus, which causes chickenpox. The cooling sensation of chamomile creams can also help calm the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and healing chickenpox affected skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with chickenpox by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions.
Apigenin: As a flavonoid present in chamomile, apigenin has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing and calming the skin affected by chickenpox.
Terpinen-4-ol: This monoterpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for chickenpox due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Seeds: They contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help reduce itching and irritation associated with chickenpox.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the severity of symptoms and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid and combine it with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Mix the combined ingredients in a double boiler until they reach a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea, the active ingredient in Kansas coneflower cream, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox.

Antiviral properties: Kansas coneflower's antiviral compounds have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause chickenpox, helping to alleviate symptoms and shorten the duration of the illness.
Immune system support: Echinacea has immunomodulatory effects, meaning it can help regulate and boost the body's immune response, which can aid in fighting off viral infections like chickenpox.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The cream's active ingredients, including alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with the rash. By promoting a healthy immune response, Echinacea angustifolia cream may help alleviate symptoms and shorten the duration of the illness, allowing the body to fight off the virus more effectively and recover faster.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and ease the discomfort associated with chickenpox, such as itching and redness.

Alkaloids (specifically, cichoric acid): Cichoric acid may have antiviral properties, which could help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Phenolic acids (specifically, chicoric acid): Chicoric acid may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with chickenpox, such as fever and headache.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin.

Roots or more specifically  Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part of Echinacea angustifolia and contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to combat the virus causing chickenpox and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried echinacea angustifolia roots with a sharp knife in late summer or early fall.
Combine the dried roots with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and infuse for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of cocoa butter and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Combine the herbal oil with the wax mixture and stir until fully incorporated to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox blisters.
Antiviral properties: Licorice extract contains antiviral compounds that may help inhibit the replication of varicella-zoster virus (VZV), reducing the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms.

Soothing and protective properties: Licorice cream's emollient and soothing properties can provide relief from itching, burning, and discomfort associated with chickenpox rash, while its anti-inflammatory compounds may also help protect the skin from further irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The soothing properties of licorice cream can help reduce the itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox blisters. Additionally, licorice root contains flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity, potentially helping to reduce the severity and duration of the chickenpox outbreak. This makes licorice cream a popular natural remedy for soothing and alleviating the symptoms of chickenpox.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms such as redness, swelling, and itching.

Licochalcone A: A chalcone compound, licochalcone A has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus, a virus closely related to the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox.

Licoricidin: A flavonoid compound, licoricidin has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and inflammation associated with chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: Their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties make them useful in treating chickenpox.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain compounds that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort of chickenpox.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing the redness and swelling associated with chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots using 500ml of ethanol at 70% for 2 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter it using a 0.2 micron filter.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax in a double boiler at 70c for 10 minutes.
Combine the wax mixture with 100g of distilled water and 50g of the extracted glycyrrhiza glabra solution.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, reduce swelling and redness associated with chickenpox blisters, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiviral properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion cream, including sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes chickenpox, helping to reduce the duration and severity of the infection.

Wound-healing properties: Dandelion cream's wound-healing compounds, such as vitamin A and fatty acids, promote skin regeneration, accelerate the healing process, and help prevent scarring from chickenpox lesions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxol, have been shown to reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms, including itching, redness, and blistering. Additionally, its soothing and calming effects on the skin help to promote healing and reduce the risk of secondary infections, making it a popular natural remedy for treating chickenpox. It also may help to alleviate the discomfort caused by the rash.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help alleviate the inflammation and itching associated with chickenpox, as flavonoids are known for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.

Saponins: Saponins may aid in reducing the severity of chickenpox symptoms, such as fever and skin lesions, by exerting an antiviral effect and promoting the clearance of the varicella-zoster virus from the skin.
Coumarins: These compounds may contribute to the antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects of Taraxacum officinale, potentially helping to reduce the duration and severity of chickenpox symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm chickenpox lesions.
Roots: They contain saponins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce itching and inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing of chickenpox lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 taraxacum officinale flowers on a sunny day and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep the cleaned flowers in 100ml of hot water for 5-7 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Mix the cooled herbal infusion with 50g of natural emollient like beeswax and 20g of shea butter.
Blend the mixture with 10ml of cold-pressed jojoba oil and 2g of vitamin e oil for antioxidant properties.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and pour it into glass jars for storage and use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds like caffeic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antihistaminic property: The cream's anti-histamine effect helps to counteract the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching, redness, and swelling, thereby reducing the severity of chickenpox symptoms like hives and rashes.

Wound-healing property: Stinging nettle creams contain bioactive compounds like silicate, which promotes collagen synthesis, wound contraction, and tissue repair, helping to accelerate the healing process of blisters and sores caused by chickenpox.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness and itching associated with the rash. By calming the skin, it provides relief from discomfort and promotes healing. Additionally, the cream's anti-viral properties may help combat the virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the duration and severity of the illness. This makes it a popular natural remedy among parents and caregivers.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica creams may help with chickenpox by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching and rash.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, specifically ursolic acid, in Urtica dioica creams may help with chickenpox by exhibiting antiviral properties, which can help combat the virus causing the infection.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, in Urtica dioica creams may help with chickenpox by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the severity of symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are the most commonly used part to make creams for chickenpox due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antiviral compounds.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are also used to make creams for chickenpox as they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are occasionally used to make creams for chickenpox, as they possess some of the same anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties found in the leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 to 50 grams of urtica dioica leaves and flowers fresh in the early morning.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing with distilled water to remove dirt.
Steep 10 grams of the cleaned urtica dioica in 250 milliliters of distilled water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix the strained liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, thereby shortening the duration and severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in lemon balm, including quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox rashes and blisters.

Soothing and calming properties: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and limonene, possess a calming effect on the skin, which can help to relieve discomfort, stress, and anxiety related to the rash and fever symptoms of chickenpox.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with chickenpox because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The soothing and calming effects of lemon balm can help to reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms, such as itching and redness. The cream's ability to boost the immune system can also aid in the body's natural healing process, helping to fight off the virus and reduce the duration of the illness. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort to those suffering from chickenpox.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms, such as inflammation and skin lesions.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help combat viral replication and alleviate symptoms associated with chickenpox, such as fever and rash.
Melissic acid: A sesquiterpene with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help prevent secondary infections and reduce the severity of skin lesions associated with chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm chickenpox symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which help reduce the risk of infection and promote healing in chickenpox lesions.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency and fragrance in early summer morning.
Steam distill the melissa officinalis mixture in a glass still under high pressure for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of melissa officinalis essential oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 50 milliliters of cold-pressed jojoba oil and 10 milliliters of glycerin to the beeswax mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with chickenpox blisters.

Wound healing properties: Comfrey's allantoin and other bioactive compounds accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and enhancing the growth of new tissue, which can aid in reducing scarring and improving the appearance of blisters.

Antiviral properties: The antimicrobial agents present in comfrey cream, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, help combat the viral infection that causes chickenpox by inhibiting viral replication and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Comfrey contains allantoin, a compound that helps to reduce redness and swelling, while also promoting wound healing. The cream's anti-inflammatory effects can help to ease the discomfort and pain associated with chickenpox, allowing the skin to heal faster. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for the skin to recover. This can lead to a faster recovery and reduced scarring.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin helps to soothe and calm skin irritations associated with chickenpox, reducing redness and inflammation.
Alkaloids (specifically, triterpenoid saponins): Alkaloids in comfrey exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with chickenpox blisters.

Polysaccharides (specifically, rhamnogalacturonan): Polysaccharides in comfrey have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the healing process of chickenpox lesions and prevent secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help soothe and calm the skin affected by chickenpox.
Stems: The stems are used for their mucilaginous properties, which help to protect the skin and reduce the risk of scarring.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the symphytum officinale roots and leaves in late summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested symphytum officinale roots and leaves in a low temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of the dried symphytum officinale roots and 2 cups of the dried leaves with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of honey with the symphytum officinale mixture.
Pour the symphytum officinale mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with chickenpox.
Antiviral: Quinine has antiviral properties that may help combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes chickenpox.

Pain relief: Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat fever, headache, and body aches associated with illnesses like chickenpox, likely due to its analgesic and antipyretic properties.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinchona pubescens, also known as Jesuit's bark, creams helps with chickenpox because it contains quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent that reduces the severity and duration of the rash. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm irritated skin, promoting healing and comfort. Additionally, Cinchona's antimicrobial properties may help to prevent secondary infections, while its anti-inflammatory properties may reduce the risk of scarring. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing chickenpox symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: This alkaloid helps reduce fever and inflammation associated with chickenpox, providing relief from symptoms like body aches and headaches.
Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of chickenpox, such as itching, redness, and swelling.

Tetrandrine: This alkaloid has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms, including fever, rash, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that may help alleviate symptoms associated with chickenpox.
Roots: May contain compounds that aid in the healing process and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona pubescens leaves and bark in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant for 2 hours.
Dry the harvested cinchona pubescens plant material in a cool dark place for 2 weeks at 25 degrees celsius.
Steep 20 grams of dried cinchona pubescens in 100ml of coconut oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Mix 10ml of the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams help with chickenpox because they contain the potent anti-inflammatory properties of Paeonia lactiflora, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to soothe and calm skin irritations. The cream's active ingredients work to reduce itching and inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with chickenpox. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to hydrate and soften the skin, promoting a faster recovery from this common childhood illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief to affected areas.

Antiviral: The peony extract in the cream exhibits antiviral properties, which can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing.

Softenening and protective: The cream's moisturizing and soothing effects help soften scabs and reduce the risk of further irritation or infection, while its protective barrier also helps shield affected skin from environmental stressors and bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing itchiness associated with chickenpox.
Rhyzomes: Containing starch and fiber, rhyzomes help to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote wound healing in cases of chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and 20g of dried aloe vera leaves.
Grind the paeonia lactiflora roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground paeonia lactiflora powder with 100ml of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then remove from heat and let cool.
Mix the cooled liquid with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of sweet almond oil to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with chickenpox because they provide soothing relief to itchy, inflamed skin. The herb marshmallow, also known as Althaea officinalis, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with the rash. Additionally, marshmallow's mucilaginous texture forms a protective barrier on the skin, shielding it from further irritation and discomfort. This natural cream helps ease the intense itching and scratching caused by chickenpox, promoting a faster and more comfortable healing process.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow cream's mucilage property helps to soothe and calm itchy, red, and irritated skin caused by chickenpox, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow cream help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chickenpox, which can lead to skin damage and scarring.

Soothing anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow cream contains various soothing anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with chickenpox lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a natural soothing agent that helps to calm inflammation and irritation associated with chickenpox.
Barks: The barks contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers and dry them for 24 hours in a warm place.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried marigold petals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered marigold petals with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 4 tablespoons of coconut oil, then heat.
Combine the mixture with 6 drops of calendula essential oil and stir until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the cooled cream into an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 30 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with chickenpox because of its soothing and calming properties, which can reduce redness, itching, and inflammation associated with this viral infection. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena cream can also help to alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by itchy blisters. Additionally, the antiviral and antimicrobial properties of lemon verbena may aid in reducing the risk of secondary bacterial infections and promoting the body's natural healing process, making it a valuable remedy for alleviating chickenpox symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon verbena cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox, providing relief to affected skin.
Antiviral property: The antiviral property of lemon verbena cream helps to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Soothing and calming property: Lemon verbena's soothing and calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety related to skin irritation and itching, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation for children and adults with chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm skin irritated by chickenpox blisters.
Seeds: The seeds of the lemon verbena plant contain antimicrobial compounds that aid in reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers for use in the cream formula.
Combine 20 milliliters of olive oil and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat until melted, stirring frequently for 30 minutes.
Mix in 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers, 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers, and 1 teaspoon of verbena officinalis infusion prepared earlier for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo creams helps with chickenpox because they possess antiviral properties that target the underlying cause of the infection, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. The cream's active ingredients work to calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing, making it an effective topical treatment for painful rashes, itching, and fever. Additionally, indigo has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent secondary infections and soothe the skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Wild Indigo cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox rashes, providing relief to affected skin.
Antiviral property: The antiviral properties of Wild Indigo cream help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Antiseptic property: The antiseptic properties of Wild Indigo cream prevent infection and promote wound healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter open sores or skin lesions caused by chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial and antiviral compounds, making them effective against the viral infection that causes chickenpox.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin rashes caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry baptisia tinctoria roots according to your preferred method.
Combine 20 grams of dried baptisia tinctoria roots with 40 milliliters of vegetable oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for 14 days by storing it in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and combine the resulting liquid with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted then remove from heat and let cool to form a thick cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with chickenpox because they provide relief from the itching, inflammation, and redness associated with this common childhood illness. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of elder flowers and leaves help to soothe the skin, reducing the discomfort and distress caused by the rash. Additionally, elder creams can aid in promoting healing and preventing infection, allowing children to recover more comfortably and quickly from chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the swelling, redness, and itching associated with chickenpox, making it more comfortable for children.
Antiviral: The antiviral compounds in elder cream can help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.
Wound-healing: Elder cream's wound-healing properties can accelerate the recovery process by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, reducing the risk of secondary infection and scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with chickenpox.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, which help to soothe itching and reduce the severity of skin lesions caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and mix them with 50ml of organic coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Add 20 drops of tea tree essential oil and stir the mixture well to combine the ingredients thoroughly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the mixture to infuse and extract the herbal properties.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Transfer the infused oil to an empty lip balm tube or small tin container and label it for future use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with the rash, making it a soothing balm for itchy skin. Additionally, St John's wort has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. This natural remedy can provide relief from the discomfort and annoyance of chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms by inhibiting the replication of the varicella-zoster virus.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in St. John's Wort creams has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe itching, redness, and swelling associated with chickenpox rash.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of phytochemical present in St. John's Wort, have antipruritic properties, which can help to relieve itching and scratching associated with chickenpox, reducing the risk of infection and secondary complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Seeds: Contain omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants that promote healing and reduce the risk of infection and scarring.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum measuring exactly one ounce.
Combine the dried flowers with one cup of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for exactly 14 days stirring occasionally every two to three days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and reserve liquid.
Mix one part of the infused liquid with three parts of beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil melt in a double boiler then pour into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with chickenpox because they provide natural relief from the intense itching and discomfort associated with this childhood illness. The cooling properties of peppermint oil have a calming effect on itchy skin, reducing the urge to scratch and preventing further irritation and infection. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe redness and swelling, making it an effective remedy for alleviating the physical discomfort of chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and menthone, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters.
Antiviral: The menthol in peppermint cream has antiviral properties that may help prevent the spread of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes chickenpox.

Sotheing and calming: Peppermint creams can provide a soothing, cooling sensation on itchy skin, reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation, allowing children to rest better during the recovery period.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other compounds, roots help soothe itching and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Barks: Containing camphor and other volatile oils, barks provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to aid in the healing of skin lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita and rinse them thoroughly in cold running water.
Dry the peppermint leaves by spreading them on paper towels for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before applying it to affected areas as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with chickenpox because they possess antiviral properties that directly target the herpes virus, which causes the outbreak. The cream's active ingredients work to reduce inflammation, itching, and blistering, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms. Additionally, maidenhair tree's natural anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe the skin, promoting healing and speeding up the recovery process. As a result, using herbal maidenhair tree creams can effectively alleviate the discomfort associated with chickenpox, allowing children to feel more comfortable and relaxed during their illness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from free radical damage, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with skin irritations or wounds.

Wound-healing properties: Ginkgo biloba has been traditionally used to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and enhancing the body's natural repair processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Contain tannins that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Crush 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 24 hours before transferring it to an airtight jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as silica and saponins, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the rash. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause secondary infections, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring. By soothing and calming the skin, field horsetail creams provide relief from the discomfort and distress caused by chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with chickenpox blisters.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of horsetail cream, including its high silica content, help combat the viral infection by inhibiting the growth of secondary bacteria that can cause further complications.

Wound-healing property: Horsetail's ability to promote collagen synthesis and improve skin elasticity aids in the healing process of chickenpox sores, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant contain silica and other minerals that help soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Rhyzomes: Field horsetail rhyzomes are used to treat skin conditions like eczema, making them a popular choice for creating creams that can help alleviate the itchy rash caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated, collecting approximately 2 ounces for processing.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry place away from direct sunlight using a food dehydrator on low setting for 3-4 hours.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using an herb grinder or mortar and pestle to release its medicinal properties.
Mix the ground equisetum arvense with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler, heating gently until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing to cool and solidify at room temperature before labeling and storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato creams helps with chickenpox because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the skin, reducing itching and redness. The natural antioxidants in wild potato also help to calm the immune response, diminishing the severity of symptoms like fever and headache. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties promote healthy wound healing, allowing blisters to crust over quickly and preventing further irritation. By providing relief from discomfort and promoting recovery, wild potato creams can make the chickenpox experience less stressful for both children and adults.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild potato cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with chickenpox lesions.
Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral properties can help combat the herpes zoster virus, which causes chickenpox, by inhibiting its replication and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Wild potato cream's emollient and soothing properties help promote wound healing, reduce discomfort, and provide relief from itching and irritation caused by chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and antioxidants, roots help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes aid in reducing redness and swelling caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh taraxacum kok saghyz leaves and chop them into small pieces within 30 minutes.
Steep the chopped leaves in 4 cups of boiling distilled water for 10-15 minutes to extract active compounds slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the solids from the liquid without delay.
Combine 1 cup of the strained liquid with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before transferring it to an air-tight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for chickenpox are topical remedies derived from plants that help alleviate the discomfort and reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms. These creams utilize the anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of various herbs to soothe itchy skin, accelerate healing, and prevent scarring. The best herbal creams for chickenpox include Calendula officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Symphytum officinale, Melaleuca alternifolia, Avena sativa, Urtica dioica, and Plantago major. If left untreated, chickenpox can lead to bacterial infections, skin damage, and in severe cases, long-term health issues and emotional distress.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chickenpox that are treated with herbal creams are shingles, eczema, and herpes simplex. These conditions involve skin lesions or rashes that can cause discomfort and pain. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as calendula, chamomile, and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help soothe and heal the skin, reducing redness, itching, and blistering.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with chickenpox is a blend of calendula, aloe vera, and tea tree oil. Calendula cream helps soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Aloe vera cream provides moisturizing and cooling properties, easing discomfort and itching. Tea tree oil, with its antimicrobial properties, helps prevent infection and promotes wound healing. Together, these creams can provide effective relief from the symptoms of chickenpox, promoting faster recovery and reduced scarring.

The cream's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and polyphenols, work to soothe itchy skin and promote wound healing. By applying the cream to affected areas, it can help to reduce the spread of the virus and alleviate discomfort, allowing the body to recover more quickly from the infection. Urtica dioica creams helps with chickenpox because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
